method: Rolling variance anomaly detector - fits scaler on train, tests on held-out test
finding: Simple rolling variance achieves AUROC=0.771 on SMD in-domain. PCA projection to 2D for cross-domain test: AUROC=0.746 (-0.025 penalty). MBA itself has AUROC=0.510 in-domain.
implication_for_a2p: A2P trains entirely within one dataset (MBA on MBA, SMD on SMD). Since anomaly patterns are fundamentally different across domains (ECG arrhythmias vs. server metric spikes), any cross-domain transfer would require learning domain-invariant anomaly representations. This is the core opportunity for JEPA-based anomaly pretraining.
verdict: Cross-domain transfer is NOT trivial. PCA projection loses 0.025 AUROC compared to in-domain detection. A2P's dataset-specific training is a fundamental limitation.
